finding: No phone number or email address anywhere on the homepage or in the footer — every contact path routes into a web form, leaving a frustrated user with no way to reach a human.

evidence.quote:
HOME
ABOUT
RESOURCES
CASE STUDIES
NEWS
CONTACT US
TERMS
PRIVACY POLICY

persona_rationale: I'm already angry and something has gone wrong. The first thing I look for is a phone number or email so I can talk to a person NOW. There is none — only a form. That is the worst possible answer when I have an urgent problem, and it would make me give up and badmouth the company.

suggested_fix.current: Footer and site offer only nav links and a contact form; no phone/email
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a visible support email (e.g. support@vendingpreneurs.com) and/or phone number to the footer and contact page, plus a stated response time

finding: Headline proof stats render as zeros, making the site look half-built and untrustworthy.

evidence.quote:
0+
ENTREPRENEURS LAUNCHED
$0M+
SNACK/DRINK SALES IN VENDINGPRENEUR MACHINES
0+
VENDING LOCATIONS W/OUR GUIDANCE

persona_rationale: I'm low on trust to begin with. Stat counters showing 0+ and $0M+ read as placeholders to me, and combined with no findable phone number I conclude I'll never be able to reach these people if something goes wrong.

suggested_fix.proposed: Populate real figures, or remove the counters until real data exists rather than shipping zeros

finding: Contact/Help is absent from the header navigation; the only path is a footer link or knowing the URL.

evidence.quote:
- navigation "Primary":
    - link "About":
    - link "Resources":
    - link "Case Studies":
    - link "News":

persona_rationale: When I'm frustrated I scan the top of the page for 'help' or 'contact' and click the first thing I see. Finding nothing in the header and having to scroll to the footer is exactly the kind of friction that tips me over the edge.

suggested_fix.current: Header nav: About / Resources / Case Studies / News only
suggested_fix.proposed: Add a 'Contact' link to the primary header navigation

finding: Application success state is a single faint green line beside the submit button — no redirect, the form is not cleared, and it is easy to miss and resubmit.

evidence.quote: Thanks. We received your details and will follow up shortly.
Automation itself missed the success state and re-submitted 5 more times — evidence of how subtle the confirmation is.

persona_rationale: When I don't see clear confirmation, I click again — and again. The test automation literally did this six times, which is exactly my behaviour. A faint line of text next to the button is not enough to tell an impatient, frustrated user that the job is done.

suggested_fix.current: Inline green text 'Thanks. We received your details and will follow up shortly.' next to button; form remains filled
suggested_fix.proposed: Redirect to /thank-you-for-applying on success (a prominent full-page confirmation) or replace the form with a large success panel and disable resubmission

finding: A finished /thank-you-for-applying confirmation page already exists in the app but the apply form never routes to it.

evidence.quote:
APPLICATION RECEIVED

THANKS FOR APPLYING.

The team reviews application details before the next conversation.
A dedicated `/thank-you-for-applying` page EXISTS in the app but the apply form does not route to it.

persona_rationale: The exact fix for my biggest apply complaint is sitting unused. A clear 'Application received' page would have stopped me resubmitting and reassured me the thing went through.

suggested_fix.current: Apply form stays in place with inline text on success
suggested_fix.proposed: On successful submit, navigate to the existing /thank-you-for-applying page

finding: No application reference or confirmation number is issued, so a user cannot quote anything when following up.

evidence.quote: Thanks. We received your details and will follow up shortly.

persona_rationale: If I have to chase this later, I want a reference number to say 'application #1234'. Without one, when I do reach someone (if I can) I have nothing to point to and the frustration starts over.

suggested_fix.current: Confirmation text with no reference identifier
suggested_fix.proposed: Show a confirmation/reference ID on the success page and include it in the confirmation email

finding: Validation relies entirely on browser-default bubbles ('Please fill out this field.') that surface one field at a time and are not tied to a styled, persistent inline label.

evidence.quote: Please fill out this field.

persona_rationale: Generic browser tooltips that pop one field at a time make me fix-and-retry repeatedly. When I'm already irritated, having errors revealed only one at a time on each submit attempt is a slow, infuriating loop.

suggested_fix.current: Browser-native validation bubble, one field per submit
suggested_fix.proposed: Show all missing/invalid fields at once with inline messages next to each field on submit

finding: The contact page offers only a web form — no phone number, no email address, no chat, and no alternative channel.

evidence.quote:
Name*
(required)
Email*
(required)
Phone
City
State
...
Message*
(required)
SEND MESSAGE

persona_rationale: I navigated to the page literally called 'Contact' to reach a human, and all I get is another box to type into and wait. For an urgent problem, a form with no phone or email fallback is not contact — it's a brush-off.

suggested_fix.proposed: Add a support email and/or phone number prominently on the contact page alongside the form

finding: The contact form has no visibly designed success or error region, carrying the same vanishing-confirmation risk seen on /apply.

evidence.quote: external-bucket form: filled for visual review, NOT submitted (outbound side effects)

persona_rationale: If submitting this contact form gives me the same faint inline text as the apply form, I won't believe my message sent and I'll send it again — or assume it failed and walk away even angrier.

suggested_fix.current: No prominent success/error state visible on the contact form
suggested_fix.proposed: Show a clear, prominent success confirmation (and a clear inline error on failure that preserves entered data) after submit

finding: No statement of when or whether anyone will reply; the prompt 'Send the team a note' sets no expectation.

evidence.quote: Send the team a note about vending locations, machine decisions, partnerships, or accelerator fit.

persona_rationale: I have no patience and no idea if this disappears into a void. Telling me 'we reply within one business day' would at least give me something to hold onto; right now I'm sending into silence.

suggested_fix.proposed: Add a response-time expectation, e.g. 'We reply within 1 business day.'

finding: The 404 page offers only a 'Back to home' link — no search, no contact/help link, and no suggestion of where content moved.

evidence.quote:
Page not found

The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has moved.

Back to home

persona_rationale: I'm already lost and frustrated, and the recovery option is to dump me back at square one. No search, no 'contact us', nothing to actually find what I was after — a dead end on top of the original problem.

suggested_fix.proposed: Add a search box and links to News, Apply, and Contact on the 404 page

finding: The 404 text says content may have 'moved' but provides no destination or link to where it moved.

evidence.quote: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has moved.

persona_rationale: If it moved, then take me there or link it. Telling me it moved and then offering nothing but 'home' is worse than saying nothing — it implies the page exists somewhere I can't reach.

suggested_fix.proposed: Either redirect known moved paths, or drop the 'or has moved' clause and offer a search + key links

finding: Legacy /blog/{slug} paths hard-404 instead of redirecting to the live /news/{slug} article that exists.

evidence.quote: 404 /blog/how-to-choose-the-perfect-location-for-vending-machine

persona_rationale: If a link I bookmarked or got in an email points at /blog/, it just breaks — and the matching article clearly exists at /news/. A broken old link with no redirect is exactly the 'confusing email led me here' scenario that put me in a bad mood in the first place.

finding: Above-the-fold on /news renders three empty card outlines before scroll (scroll-reveal animation), so first paint looks broken.

evidence.quote: Above-the-fold on /news shows three empty card outlines before any scrolling (see news-001-load.png) — first paint looks broken/unfinished.

persona_rationale: I already assume things are broken. Landing on a page of empty outlines confirms my suspicion before I even scroll, and I'm primed to leave.

suggested_fix.current: Article cards hidden until scrolled into view, leaving empty outlines on first paint
suggested_fix.proposed: Render the first row of cards visible by default; only apply scroll-reveal to content below the fold

finding: The section is labelled 'NEWS' in the nav and URL but the page heading reads 'BLOG' — the same section under two names.

evidence.quote: Page is headed "BLOG" while the nav item that leads here says "NEWS" and the route is /news. Same section, two names.

persona_rationale: When I'm irritated, inconsistency makes me wonder if I clicked the wrong thing. 'News' then 'Blog' makes me second-guess where I am.

suggested_fix.proposed: Use one label consistently — make the page heading 'NEWS' to match the nav and URL

finding: A news thumbnail image returns 500 on load, leaving a broken image and a console error.

evidence.quote: 500 /_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fcdn.prod.website-files.com%2F...png&w=640&q=75

persona_rationale: A broken image is one more thing that screams 'this site doesn't work', reinforcing my expectation that I'll have trouble here too.

suggested_fix.current: Thumbnail proxied through /_next/image fails with 500 (dead CDN source)
suggested_fix.proposed: Fix or replace the broken source asset and add a fallback image so a failed load doesn't show a broken/blank tile

finding: A 'Schedule failed' filter is exposed in the workflow navigation with no inline explanation of what failed or how to recover.

evidence.quote:
Scheduled
Schedule failed

persona_rationale: As a frustrated admin coming in to fix something, seeing 'Schedule failed' staring at me with no detail about what failed or how to retry is salt in the wound — it tells me something broke but gives me nothing to act on.

suggested_fix.current: 'Schedule failed' filter link with no context
suggested_fix.proposed: When the filter has results, show what failed and a Retry action per item; hide or de-emphasise the filter when there are zero failures

finding: Password reset copy hedges ('If it has admin access, we'll send a password reset link') and no on-screen confirmation flow was observed, so a locked-out admin can't tell whether the email is coming.

evidence.quote: Enter your Studio email. If it has admin access, we'll send a password reset link.

persona_rationale: If I'm locked out and already angry, I need to KNOW the reset email is on its way. A conditional 'if it has admin access' with no clear post-submit confirmation leaves me wondering whether anything happened.

suggested_fix.current: 'If it has admin access, we'll send a password reset link' with no confirmed success state
suggested_fix.proposed: After submit, show a clear confirmation: 'If that email has admin access, a reset link is on its way — check your inbox and spam.'
